rationale: Both correctly diagnose the config/tokenizer mismatch and give the same verified workaround, matching the thread's core resolution. X is more grounded, citing specific line ranges and pulling explicit evidence from both YAML headers to explain the two-stage vs single-stage topology, and its disposition (close this bug, track hardening in a follow-up issue) matches Gaohan123's actual request more precisely than Y's 'keep-open' framing. Y is solid and well-structured but slightly thinner on cited evidence and less precise about the issue's actual disposition.
